role: Secretary of the Supreme National Security Council (2007–2013); presidential candidate
bio: Saeed Jalili is an Iranian hardline politician who served as secretary of the [Supreme National Security Council](/orgs/supreme-national-security-council) from 2007 to 2013, appointed by [Mahmoud Ahmadinejad](/people/mahmoud-ahmadinejad). He was Iran's chief nuclear negotiator during that period, taking a confrontational stance toward Western powers. He ran unsuccessfully in the 2013 presidential election and was a leading candidate in the [2024 snap presidential election](/events/presidential-election-iran-2024-snap), finishing second to [Masoud Pezeshkian](/people/masoud-pezeshkian) in the runoff. He is closely aligned with the [Front of Islamic Revolution Stability](/orgs/front-of-islamic-revolution-stability) and was endorsed by [Mohammad-Taqi Mesbah-Yazdi](/people/mohammad-taqi-mesbah-yazdi). He lost part of his right leg during the [Iran–Iraq War](/events/iran-iraq-war).
editorial_notes: Lost right leg in Iran-Iraq War. Ran in 2013 and 2024 presidential elections. Also participated in 2021 election.
